title: Anthropic leases 16-floor Manhattan office, plans to double NYC headcount by year-end
subtitle: The lease at 330 Hudson Street signals that frontier AI hiring is no longer a Bay-Area-only story.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic confirmed on July 17, 2026 that it has leased the full 16 floors of 330 Hudson Street in Manhattan's Hudson Square — roughly 465,000 square feet with capacity for about 1,700 desks — and plans to double its New York headcount to over 1,000 employees by the end of 2026. New York is already Anthropic's second-largest office, behind San Francisco.

The move is a signal that the frontier-lab talent market is no longer geographically concentrated. Palantir, OpenAI and EliseAI have all expanded in Manhattan in the past year. The pull is not proximity to models — it is proximity to enterprise buyers, especially in finance, media and legal, three sectors where Anthropic's Claude for Business and Harvey partnerships have been landing.

The scale is also a live indicator of frontier-lab burn. Anthropic started 2026 with under 500 NYC staff and is doubling in twelve months while simultaneously negotiating a $10 billion compute lease with Meta and hiring senior operators away from Google DeepMind and Monzo. The company is spending like it is preparing for a 2027 IPO — and hiring like a firm that expects revenue to justify it.

For learners outside California: this is good news. Frontier AI jobs — research, applied engineering, product, safety, policy — are opening in New York, London, Dublin, Tokyo, and increasingly Seoul. If you are picking where to build a career in AI, the map is now bigger than a strip of highway between San Francisco and Palo Alto. Look at where labs are signing long-term leases, not just where they were founded.
